tasks.title: Add exterior texture support to asset collection config and admin panel
tasks.description: Currently, the Admin Panel asset collection manager has no way to set exterior textures the way interior textures are set. (1) Add wallTextureId and roofTextureId fields to the BuildingConfigurationPanel exterior tab so users can select wall and roof textures per building type and per category preset. (2) Add texture selection dropdowns (not the Visual Assets Browser) for exterior wall texture, roof texture, door texture, and window trim texture. (3) Ensure these texture IDs are persisted in the WorldTypeCollectionConfig.buildingConfig and flow through to ProceduralBuildingGenerator. (4) Add fields for balcony texture, ironwork balcony texture, front porch texture, and shutter texture to ProceduralStylePreset in shared/game-engine/types.ts. (5) Add corresponding dropdown selectors in the admin panel exterior tab for these feature textures. (6) Ensure the texture IDs are stored and loaded correctly from the asset collection.

tasks.title: Fix exterior texture application in ProceduralBuildingGenerator
tasks.description: Even when wall/roof texture IDs are manually set, textures do not appear in the preview or in-game. (1) In ProceduralBuildingGenerator.ts, trace the texture loading path from wallTextureId/roofTextureId in BuildingStyle through to actual Babylon.js StandardMaterial.diffuseTexture assignment. (2) Fix the texture loading pipeline: ensure that when a textureId is provided, it resolves to an actual asset URL via AssetCollectionLoader, downloads the texture, and applies it to the building mesh material. (3) Add fallback: if texture loading fails, fall back to solid color. (4) Fix the material cache key to include texture IDs so different textures don't get the same cached material. (5) Test with multiple building types to confirm textures appear correctly on walls, roofs, doors, and windows.

tasks.title: Implement balcony, ironwork, porch, and shutter texture application
tasks.description: When buildings have balconies, ironwork balconies, front porches, or shutters, these features should use their configured textures. (1) In ProceduralBuildingGenerator.ts, locate the mesh generation code for balconies (hasBalcony), ironwork balconies (hasIronworkBalcony), porches (hasPorch), and shutters (hasShutters). (2) When generating these features, check if a corresponding texture ID exists in the style preset (balconyTextureId, ironworkBalconyTextureId, porchTextureId, shutterTextureId). (3) If a texture ID exists, load the texture from the asset collection and apply it to the feature's material. (4) If no texture is set, continue using the current procedural color approach. (5) Ensure the preview in BuildingConfigurationPanel also applies these textures.

tasks.title: Create varied exterior style presets per building category
tasks.description: Currently, building exteriors across categories look remarkably similar. Create distinct default style presets for each building category. (1) In shared/game-engine/building-defaults.ts or a new building-style-presets.ts file, define at least 3-5 varied ProceduralStylePreset options per category: commercial_food (warm tones, large windows, awnings), commercial_retail (display windows, signage areas, varied facades), commercial_service (professional appearances, muted tones), civic (grand entrances, taller structures, formal materials), industrial (utilitarian, metal/brick, loading doors), maritime (weathered wood, nautical elements), residential (varied colors, residential features like porches/shutters). (2) Each preset should vary materialType, architectureStyle, roofStyle, color palettes, and feature flags. (3) Within each category, presets should be visually distinct from each other. (4) Update the ProceduralBuildingGenerator to randomly select from category-specific presets when no explicit preset is configured.

tasks.title: Create varied exterior style presets per building subtype
tasks.description: Beyond category-level variety, individual building subtypes should have distinctive appearances. (1) Define subtype-specific style hints for at least the most common subtypes: Restaurant (warm lighting, large front windows), Bar (darker tones, smaller windows), Bank (stone/marble, grand entrance), Hotel (multi-story, balconies), Church (tall, steeple, stained glass hints), School (institutional, wide), Hospital (white/clean, wide), Blacksmith (stone base, chimney, industrial), Farm (wood, low, wide), Bakery (warm colors, chimney, display window). (2) Create a subtype style override system that layers on top of category presets. (3) In ProceduralBuildingGenerator, when generating a building, apply subtype-specific overrides (extra chimneys, forced balconies, specific material types, color adjustments) on top of the category preset. (4) Ensure the variety is noticeable but still cohesive within a settlement's overall style.

tasks.title: Diversify interior layout templates per building category
tasks.description: All interior layout templates currently look more or less the same. Build out realistic, varied interior layouts for each building category. (1) In BuildingInteriorGenerator.ts, create distinct InteriorLayoutTemplate definitions for each category. For commercial_food: open kitchen area with counter/bar, dining floor with tables, storage room, possibly upstairs office or seating. For commercial_retail: display area with shelves/racks in front, counter/register area, back storage room. For commercial_service: reception/waiting area, multiple office/service rooms, possible vault or equipment room. For civic: large public hall or nave, administrative offices, meeting rooms. For industrial: large open workshop/factory floor, tool storage, small office. For maritime: docks office, warehouse storage, navigation room. For residential: living room, kitchen, bedrooms upstairs, bathroom. (2) Each category should have at least 2-3 layout template variants. (3) Vary room sizes, counts, and arrangements between variants. (4) Ensure furniture presets match the room functions (e.g., restaurant kitchen gets stoves/prep tables, shop floor gets display shelves).

tasks.title: Create subtype-specific interior layout templates
tasks.description: Within each category, subtypes should have specialized interiors. (1) Create subtype-specific layout templates for key building subtypes: Restaurant (kitchen, dining hall, bar area), Bar (large bar counter, seating, cellar), Bakery (ovens/prep area, display counter, small seating), Bank (lobby with teller windows, vault, offices), Hotel (lobby/reception, multiple guest rooms, dining room), Church (nave with pews, altar, vestry, bell tower room), School (classrooms, library, office), Hospital (waiting room, exam rooms, ward), Blacksmith (forge area with anvil, storage, shop front), Farm (barn with stalls, tool shed, farmhouse interior). (2) Each subtype template should have unique room functions, furniture placements, and dimensions that make the interior immediately recognizable. (3) Register all templates in a template registry keyed by building subtype so BuildingInteriorGenerator can select the right one.

tasks.title: Fix interior texture application from asset collection config
tasks.description: Interior textures set in the admin panel do not appear in the preview or in the game. (1) In BuildingInteriorGenerator.ts, trace the texture loading path for wallTextureId, floorTextureId, and ceilingTextureId from InteriorTemplateConfig through to actual material assignment. (2) Ensure the texture IDs are resolved via AssetCollectionLoader to actual texture URLs. (3) Fix the material creation to load and apply textures when IDs are provided. (4) Add proper UV mapping for interior surfaces so textures display correctly. (5) Test with different texture assets to confirm walls, floors, and ceilings all display their configured textures. (6) Ensure the ConfigDetailPanel preview also renders these textures correctly.

tasks.title: Fix furniture set selection to actually populate different furniture
tasks.description: Choosing a furniture set in the admin panel currently seems to do nothing. (1) Trace the furniture set selection from the admin panel (BuildingConfigurationPanel or ConfigDetailPanel) through to BuildingInteriorGenerator and FurnitureModelLoader. (2) Verify that the selected furnitureSet name in InteriorTemplateConfig is being read and used when generating interior furniture. (3) Each furniture set (tavern, shop, residential, church, etc.) should produce visually distinct furniture layouts. (4) Fix the connection so that when a user selects 'tavern' set, the interior gets bar counters, stools, and tables; when 'shop' is selected, it gets display shelves and a counter; when 'residential' is selected, it gets couches, beds, and dining tables. (5) Ensure the preview in the admin panel updates when a different furniture set is selected. (6) If furniture models are missing for certain sets, add placeholder geometry with appropriate labeling.

tasks.title: Fix lighting preset application in interiors
tasks.description: Selecting a lighting preset in the admin panel seems to have no effect. (1) Trace the lighting preset selection from the admin panel through to BuildingInteriorGenerator's interior lighting setup. (2) In BuildingInteriorGenerator, verify that the lightingPreset from InteriorTemplateConfig is read and applied when creating interior lights. (3) Each preset should produce a distinctly different atmosphere: 'bright' (high intensity white light), 'dim' (low intensity), 'warm' (amber/orange tint, medium intensity), 'cool' (blue-white tint), 'candlelit' (very warm orange, low intensity, flickering effect if possible). (4) Ensure the hemispheric light ambientIntensity, point light intensity, and light colors all change based on the preset. (5) Verify the preview reflects the selected lighting preset. (6) Test transitioning between presets to ensure lights are properly cleaned up and recreated.

tasks.title: Add exterior preview rendering in BuildingConfigurationPanel
tasks.description: The admin panel should show an accurate 3D preview of the building exterior with applied textures, colors, and features. (1) In ConfigDetailPanel.tsx or BuildingConfigurationPanel.tsx, ensure the Babylon.js preview scene renders a building mesh using the ProceduralBuildingGenerator with the current exterior configuration. (2) The preview should reflect: wall color/texture, roof color/texture, window style, door style, balcony/porch/shutters if enabled, correct floor count, and correct building dimensions. (3) When the user changes any exterior setting, the preview should update in real-time. (4) Ensure the preview camera is positioned to show the full building from a 3/4 angle. (5) Add orbit controls so the user can rotate around the preview building.
